campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss several community improvements. They accepted a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help fund youth recreation scholarships, and they're moving forward with plans to build a bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue by applying for grant funding.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen for youth scholarships
Bicycle and pedestrian bridge planned over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant application being submitted for the Hamilton Avenue crossing project
Extension of interim ordinance 2326 under consideration

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 4, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council met to decide which organizations would receive grant money for community events and activities. They approved specific funding amounts for selected groups and updated the rules for how organizations can apply for and use event grants in the future.
keyTopics: Approved grant recipients and how much money each organization will receive
Set funding allocation for the Competitive Event Grant Program for the upcoming fiscal year
Updated and clarified rules for Community Event and Street Closure Grants

san-jose.title: San José City Council — March 25, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting on March 25, 2026 provided information about how residents could watch the proceedings. The meeting offered live translations in over 50 languages and multiple ways for the public to tune in, though no public comment was accepted during this particular session.
keyTopics: Live translation services available in 50+ languages
Multiple viewing options: cable, YouTube, website, and Zoom
Phone dial-in access for residents without internet
No public comment period scheduled for this meeting

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met on March 30, 2026 to approve meeting minutes and hear public comments. The council reviewed various committee reports and set upcoming meeting schedules for city boards and commissions.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents
Committee and staff reports
Scheduling for boards and commissions meetings
Agendas available at City Clerk's Office (500 Castro Street)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grants that help fund affordable housing and community programs in Sunnyvale. The commission discussed which organizations should receive Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) money to support local housing and services for residents in need.
keyTopics: Federal grant funding decisions for affordable housing projects
Review of Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) applicants
Approval of previous meeting minutes from February

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council focused on helping local businesses grow and succeed. They discussed a program to forgive unpaid business licenses for shops willing to get current, launched a campaign to encourage residents to shop locally during summer events, and explored ways to attract new retail stores and startups to Cupertino through partnerships and financial incentives.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - forgiveness for unpaid licenses at local shopping centers
Summer Shop Local Campaign - promotional events to support Cupertino businesses
Economic incentives for retail stores and startups to set up in the city
Public-Private Partnerships to sponsor city events and boost local commerce

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — March 30, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's City Council held a Governance and Ethics Committee meeting on March 30, 2026. The city is continuing to allow residents to attend meetings either in person or remotely via Zoom or phone.
keyTopics: Governance and Ethics Committee meetings held in-person
Remote participation options available via Zoom and phone
Public can submit written comments by email before meetings
Multiple ways for residents to engage with city government

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026 to recognize Women's History Month and approve two agreements. They voted to allow the American Red Cross to use city facilities during emergencies, and approved a three-year contract with Insight Public Sector to provide email security and backup services for about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Red Cross disaster relief facility agreement
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late packet report added to agenda
